NKJV.1: Now it happened on one of those days, as He taught the people in the temple and preached the gospel, that the chief priests and the scribes, together with the elders, confronted Him
NKJV.2: and spoke to Him, saying, “Tell us, by what authority are You doing these things? Or who is he who gave You this authority?”
NKJV.3: But He answered and said to them, “I also will ask you one thing, and answer Me:
NKJV.4: The  baptism of John—was it from heaven or from men?”
NKJV.5: And they reasoned among themselves, saying, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ He will say, ‘Why then did you not believe him?’
NKJV.6: But if we say, ‘From men,’ all the people will stone us, for they are persuaded that John was a prophet.”
NKJV.7: So they answered that they did not know where it was from.
NKJV.8: And Jesus said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things.”
NKJV.9: Then He began to tell the people this parable: “A certain man planted a vineyard, leased it to vinedressers, and went into a far country for a long time.
NKJV.10: Now at vintage-time he sent a servant to the vinedressers, that they might give him some of the fruit of the vineyard. But the vinedressers beat him and sent him away empty-handed.
NKJV.11: Again he sent another servant; and they beat him also, treated him shamefully, and sent him away empty-handed.
NKJV.12: And again he sent a third; and they wounded him also and cast him out.
NKJV.13: “Then the owner of the vineyard said, ‘What shall I do? I will send my beloved son. Probably they will respect him when they see him.’
NKJV.14: But when the vinedressers saw him, they reasoned among themselves, saying, ‘This is the  heir. Come,  let us kill him, that the inheritance may be  ours.’
NKJV.15: So they cast him out of the vineyard and killed him. Therefore what will the owner of the vineyard do to them?
NKJV.16: He will come and destroy those vinedressers and give the vineyard to others.” And when they heard it they said, “Certainly not!”
NKJV.17: Then He looked at them and said, “What then is this that is written:  ‘The stone which the builders rejected Has become the chief cornerstone’?
NKJV.18: Whoever falls on that stone will be  broken; but  on whomever it falls, it will grind him to powder.”
NKJV.19: And the chief priests and the scribes that very hour sought to lay hands on Him, but they feared the people —for they knew He had spoken this parable against them.
NKJV.20: So they watched Him, and sent spies who pretended to be righteous, that they might seize on His words, in order to deliver Him to the power and the authority of the governor.
NKJV.21: Then they asked Him, saying, “Teacher, we know that You say and teach rightly, and You do not show personal favoritism, but teach the way of God in truth:
NKJV.22: Is it lawful for us to pay taxes to Caesar or not?”
NKJV.23: But He perceived their craftiness, and said to them, “Why do you test Me?
NKJV.24: Show Me a denarius. Whose image and inscription does it have?” They answered and said, “Caesar’s.”
NKJV.25: And He said to them, “Render therefore to Caesar the things that are Caesar’s, and to God the things that are God’s.”
NKJV.26: But they could not catch Him in His words in the presence of the people. And they marveled at His answer and kept silent.
NKJV.27: Then some of the Sadducees, who deny that there is a resurrection, came to Him and asked Him,
NKJV.28: saying: “Teacher, Moses wrote to us that if a man’s brother dies, having a wife, and he dies without children, his brother should take his wife and raise up offspring for his brother.
NKJV.29: Now there were seven brothers. And the first took a wife, and died without children.
NKJV.30: And the second took her as wife, and he died childless.
NKJV.31: Then the third took her, and in like manner the seven also; and they left no children, and died.
NKJV.32: Last of all the woman died also.
NKJV.33: Therefore, in the resurrection, whose wife does she become? For all seven had her as wife.”
NKJV.34: Jesus answered and said to them, “The sons of this age marry and are given in marriage.
NKJV.35: But those who are  counted worthy to attain that age, and the resurrection from the dead, neither marry nor are given in marriage;
NKJV.36: nor can they die anymore, for  they are equal to the angels and are sons of God,  being sons of the resurrection.
NKJV.37: But even Moses showed in the burning bush passage that the dead are raised, when he called the Lord ‘the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.’
NKJV.38: For He is not the God of the dead but of the living, for  all live to Him.”
NKJV.39: Then some of the scribes answered and said, “Teacher, You have spoken well.”
NKJV.40: But after that they dared not question Him anymore.
NKJV.41: And He said to them, “How can they say that the Christ is the Son of David?
NKJV.42: Now David himself said in the Book of Psalms: ‘The LORD said to my Lord, “Sit at My right hand,
NKJV.43: Till I make Your enemies Your footstool.” ’
NKJV.44: Therefore David calls Him ‘Lord’;  how is He then his Son?”
NKJV.45: Then, in the hearing of all the people, He said to His disciples,
NKJV.46: “Beware of the scribes, who desire to go around in long robes,  love greetings in the marketplaces, the best seats in the synagogues, and the best places at feasts,
NKJV.47: who devour widows’ houses, and for a  pretense make long prayers. These will receive greater condemnation.”

ESV.5: And they discussed it with one another, saying, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will say, ‘Why did you not believe him?’
ESV.6: But if we say, ‘From man,’ all the people will stone us to death, for they are convinced that John was a prophet.”
ESV.7: So they answered that they did not know where it came from.
ESV.8: And Jesus said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things.”
ESV.9: And he began to tell the people this parable: “A man planted a vineyard and let it out to tenants and went into another country for a long while.
ESV.10: When the time came, he sent a servant  to the tenants, so that they would give him some of the fruit of the vineyard. But the tenants beat him and sent him away empty-handed.
ESV.11: And he sent another servant. But they also beat and treated him shamefully, and sent him away empty-handed.
ESV.12: And he sent yet a third. This one also they wounded and cast out.
ESV.13: Then the owner of the vineyard said, ‘What shall I do? I will send my beloved son; perhaps they will respect him.’
ESV.14: But when the tenants saw him, they said to themselves, ‘This is the heir. Let us kill him, so that the inheritance may be ours.’
ESV.15: And they threw him out of the vineyard and killed him. What then will the owner of the vineyard do to them?
ESV.16: He will come and destroy those tenants and give the vineyard to others.” When they heard this, they said, “Surely not!”
ESV.17: But he looked directly at them and said, “What then is this that is written:  “‘The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone’?
ESV.18: Everyone who falls on that stone will be broken to pieces, and when it falls on anyone, it will crush him.”
ESV.19: The scribes and the chief priests sought to lay hands on him at that very hour, for they perceived that he had told this parable against them, but they feared the people.
ESV.20: So they watched him and sent spies, who pretended to be sincere, that they might catch him in something he said, so as to deliver him up to the authority and jurisdiction of the governor.
ESV.21: So they asked him, “Teacher, we know that you speak and teach rightly, and show no partiality,  but truly teach the way of God.
ESV.22: Is it lawful for us to give tribute to Caesar, or not?”
ESV.23: But he perceived their craftiness, and said to them,
ESV.24: “Show me a denarius.  Whose likeness and inscription does it have?” They said, “Caesar's.”
ESV.25: He said to them, “Then render to Caesar the things that are Caesar's, and to God the things that are God's.”
ESV.26: And they were not able in the presence of the people to catch him in what he said, but marveling at his answer they became silent.
ESV.27: There came to him some Sadducees, those who deny that there is a resurrection,
ESV.28: and they asked him a question, saying, “Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a man's brother dies, having a wife but no children, the man  must take the widow and raise up offspring for his brother.
ESV.29: Now there were seven brothers. The first took a wife, and died without children.
ESV.31: and the third took her, and likewise all seven left no children and died.
ESV.33: In the resurrection, therefore, whose wife will the woman be? For the seven had her as wife.”
ESV.34: And Jesus said to them,  “The sons of this age marry and are given in marriage,
ESV.35: but those who are considered worthy to attain to that age and to the resurrection from the dead neither marry nor are given in marriage,
ESV.36: for they cannot die anymore, because they are equal to angels and are sons of God, being sons  of the resurrection.
ESV.37: But that the dead are raised, even Moses showed, in the passage about the bush, where he calls the Lord the God of Abraham and the God of Isaac and the God of Jacob.
ESV.38: Now he is not God of the dead, but of the living, for all live to him.”
ESV.39: Then some of the scribes answered, “Teacher, you have spoken well.”
ESV.40: For they no longer dared to ask him any question.
ESV.41: But he said to them, “How can they say that the Christ is David's son?
ESV.42: For David himself says in the Book of Psalms,  “‘The Lord said to my Lord, “Sit at my right hand,
ESV.43: until I make your enemies your footstool.”’
ESV.44: David thus calls him Lord, so how is he his son?”
ESV.45: And in the hearing of all the people he said to his disciples,
ESV.46: “Beware of the scribes, who like to walk around in long robes, and love greetings in the marketplaces and the best seats in the synagogues and the places of honor at feasts,
ESV.47: who devour widows' houses and for a pretense make long prayers. They will receive the greater condemnation.”

NASB.1: On one of the days while He was teaching the people in the temple and preaching the gospel, the chief priests and the scribes with the elders confronted [Him],
NASB.2: and they spoke, saying to Him, 'Tell us by what authority You are doing these things, or who is the one who gave You this authority?'

NASB.4: 'Was the baptism of John from heaven or from men?'
NASB.5: They reasoned among themselves, saying, 'If we say, 'From heaven,' He will say, 'Why did you not believe him?'
NASB.6: 'But if we say, 'From men,' all the people will stone us to death, for they are convinced that John was a prophet.'
NASB.7: So they answered that they did not know where [it came] from.
NASB.8: And Jesus said to them, 'Nor will I tell you by what authority I do these things.'
NASB.9: And He began to tell the people this parable: 'A man planted a vineyard and rented it out to vine-growers, and went on a journey for a long time.
NASB.10: 'At the [harvest] time he sent a slave to the vine-growers, so that they would give him [some] of the produce of the vineyard; but the vine-growers beat him and sent him away empty-handed.
NASB.11: 'And he proceeded to send another slave; and they beat him also and treated him shamefully and sent him away empty-handed.
NASB.12: 'And he proceeded to send a third; and this one also they wounded and cast out.
NASB.13: 'The owner of the vineyard said, 'What shall I do? I will send my beloved son; perhaps they will respect him.'
NASB.14: 'But when the vine-growers saw him, they reasoned with one another, saying, 'This is the heir; let us kill him so that the inheritance will be ours.'
NASB.15: 'So they threw him out of the vineyard and killed him. What, then, will the owner of the vineyard do to them?
NASB.16: 'He will come and destroy these vine-growers and will give the vineyard to others.' When they heard it, they said, 'May it never be!'
NASB.17: But Jesus looked at them and said, 'What then is this that is written: 'THE STONE WHICH THE BUILDERS REJECTED, THIS BECAME THE CHIEF CORNER [stone]'?
NASB.18: 'Everyone who falls on that stone will be broken to pieces; but on whomever it falls, it will scatter him like dust.'
NASB.19: The scribes and the chief priests tried to lay hands on Him that very hour, and they feared the people; for they understood that He spoke this parable against them.

NASB.22: 'Is it lawful for us to pay taxes to Caesar, or not?'
NASB.23: But He detected their trickery and said to them,

NASB.31: and the third married her; and in the same way all seven died, leaving no children.
NASB.33: 'In the resurrection therefore, which one's wife will she be? For all seven had married her.'

NASB.37: 'But that the dead are raised, even Moses showed, in the [passage about the burning] bush, where he calls the Lord THE GOD OF ABRAHAM, AND THE GOD OF ISAAC, AND THE GOD OF JACOB.
NASB.38: 'Now He is not the God of the dead but of the living; for all live to Him.'
NASB.39: Some of the scribes answered and said, 'Teacher, You have spoken well.'
NASB.40: For they did not have courage to question Him any longer about anything.
NASB.41: Then He said to them, 'How [is it that] they say the Christ is David's son?
NASB.42: 'For David himself says in the book of Psalms, 'THE LORD SAID TO MY LORD, 'SIT AT MY RIGHT HAND,
NASB.43: UNTIL I MAKE YOUR ENEMIES A FOOTSTOOL FOR YOUR FEET.''
NASB.44: 'Therefore David calls Him 'Lord,' and how is He his son?'
NASB.45: And while all the people were listening, He said to the disciples,
NASB.46: 'Beware of the scribes, who like to walk around in long robes, and love respectful greetings in the market places, and chief seats in the synagogues and places of honor at banquets,

NLT.1: One day as Jesus was teaching the people and preaching the Good News in the Temple, the leading priests, the teachers of religious law, and the elders came up to him.
NLT.2: They demanded, “By what authority are you doing all these things? Who gave you the right?”
NLT.3: “Let me ask you a question first,” he replied.
NLT.4: “Did John’s authority to baptize come from heaven, or was it merely human?”
NLT.5: They talked it over among themselves. “If we say it was from heaven, he will ask why we didn’t believe John.
NLT.6: But if we say it was merely human, the people will stone us because they are convinced John was a prophet.”
NLT.7: So they finally replied that they didn’t know.
NLT.8: And Jesus responded, “Then I won’t tell you by what authority I do these things.”
NLT.9: Now Jesus turned to the people again and told them this story: “A man planted a vineyard, leased it to tenant farmers, and moved to another country to live for several years.
NLT.10: At the time of the grape harvest, he sent one of his servants to collect his share of the crop. But the farmers attacked the servant, beat him up, and sent him back empty-handed.
NLT.11: So the owner sent another servant, but they also insulted him, beat him up, and sent him away empty-handed.
NLT.12: A third man was sent, and they wounded him and chased him away.
NLT.13: “‘What will I do?’ the owner asked himself. ‘I know! I’ll send my cherished son. Surely they will respect him.’
NLT.14: “But when the tenant farmers saw his son, they said to each other, ‘Here comes the heir to this estate. Let’s kill him and get the estate for ourselves!’
NLT.15: So they dragged him out of the vineyard and murdered him. “What do you suppose the owner of the vineyard will do to them?” Jesus asked.
NLT.16: “I’ll tell you — he will come and kill those farmers and lease the vineyard to others.” “How terrible that such a thing should ever happen,” his listeners protested.
NLT.17:
Jesus looked at them and said, “Then what does this Scripture mean? ‘The stone that the builders rejected
has now become the cornerstone.’
NLT.18: Everyone who stumbles over that stone will be broken to pieces, and it will crush anyone it falls on.”
NLT.19: The teachers of religious law and the leading priests wanted to arrest Jesus immediately because they realized he was telling the story against them — they were the wicked farmers. But they were afraid of the people’s reaction.
NLT.20: Watching for their opportunity, the leaders sent spies pretending to be honest men. They tried to get Jesus to say something that could be reported to the Roman governor so he would arrest Jesus.
NLT.21: “Teacher,” they said, “we know that you speak and teach what is right and are not influenced by what others think. You teach the way of God truthfully.
NLT.22: Now tell us — is it right for us to pay taxes to Caesar or not?”
NLT.23: He saw through their trickery and said,
NLT.24: “Show me a Roman coin. Whose picture and title are stamped on it?” “Caesar’s,” they replied.
NLT.25: “Well then,” he said, “give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar, and give to God what belongs to God.”
NLT.26: So they failed to trap him by what he said in front of the people. Instead, they were amazed by his answer, and they became silent.
NLT.27: Then Jesus was approached by some Sadducees — religious leaders who say there is no resurrection from the dead.
NLT.28: They posed this question: “Teacher, Moses gave us a law that if a man dies, leaving a wife but no children, his brother should marry the widow and have a child who will carry on the brother’s name.
NLT.29: Well, suppose there were seven brothers. The oldest one married and then died without children.
NLT.30: So the second brother married the widow, but he also died.
NLT.31: Then the third brother married her. This continued with all seven of them, who died without children.
NLT.33: So tell us, whose wife will she be in the resurrection? For all seven were married to her!”
NLT.34: Jesus replied, “Marriage is for people here on earth.
NLT.35: But in the age to come, those worthy of being raised from the dead will neither marry nor be given in marriage.
NLT.36: And they will never die again. In this respect they will be like angels. They are children of God and children of the resurrection.
NLT.37: “But now, as to whether the dead will be raised — even Moses proved this when he wrote about the burning bush. Long after Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob had died, he referred to the Lord as ‘the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.’
NLT.38: So he is the God of the living, not the dead, for they are all alive to him.”
NLT.39: “Well said, Teacher!” remarked some of the teachers of religious law who were standing there.
NLT.40: And then no one dared to ask him any more questions.
NLT.41: Then Jesus presented them with a question. “Why is it,” he asked, “that the Messiah is said to be the son of David?
NLT.42:
For David himself wrote in the book of Psalms: ‘The LORD said to my Lord,
Sit in the place of honor at my right hand
NLT.43:
until I humble your enemies,
making them a footstool under your feet.’
NLT.44: Since David called the Messiah ‘Lord,’ how can the Messiah be his son?”
NLT.45: Then, with the crowds listening, he turned to his disciples and said,
NLT.46: “Beware of these teachers of religious law! For they like to parade around in flowing robes and love to receive respectful greetings as they walk in the marketplaces. And how they love the seats of honor in the synagogues and the head table at banquets.
NLT.47: Yet they shamelessly cheat widows out of their property and then pretend to be pious by making long prayers in public. Because of this, they will be severely punished.”

WEB.39: Some of the scribes answered, “Teacher, you speak well.”

WEB.41: He said to them, “Why do they say that the Christ is David’s son?

WEB.43: until I make your enemies the footstool of your feet.”’
WEB.44: “David therefore calls him Lord, so how is he his son?”
WEB.45: In the hearing of all the people, he said to his disciples,
WEB.46: “Beware of the scribes, who like to walk in long robes, and love greetings in the marketplaces, the best seats in the synagogues, and the best places at feasts;
WEB.47: who devour widows’ houses, and for a pretense make long prayers: these will receive greater condemnation.”

AMP.1: On one of the days, as Jesus was instructing the people in the temple [area] and preaching the good news (gospel), the chief priests and the scribes along with the elders confronted Him,
AMP.2: and said to Him, "Tell us by what kind of authority You are doing these things? Or who is the one who gave You this authority?"
AMP.3: Jesus replied, "I will also ask you a question. You tell Me:
AMP.4: The baptism of John [the Baptist]—was it from heaven [that is, ordained by God] or from men?"
AMP.5: They discussed and debated it among themselves, saying, "If we say, 'From heaven,' He will say, 'Why did you not believe him?'
AMP.6: But if we say, 'From men,' all the people will stone us to death, for they are firmly convinced that John was a prophet."
AMP.7: So they replied that they did not know from where it came.
AMP.8: Then Jesus said to them, "Nor am I telling you by what [kind of] authority I do these things."
AMP.9: Then He began to tell the people this parable: "A man planted a vineyard and leased it to tenant farmers, and went on a journey for a long time [to another country].
AMP.10: At harvest time he sent a servant [as his representative] to the tenants, so that they would give him his share of the fruit of the vineyard; but the tenants beat the servant and sent him away empty-handed.
AMP.11: So he again sent another servant; they also beat him and dishonored and treated him disgracefully and sent him away empty-handed.
AMP.12: And he sent yet a third; and this one too they wounded and threw out [of the vineyard].
AMP.13: Then the owner of the vineyard said, 'What shall I do? I will send my beloved son; perhaps they will have respect for him.'
AMP.14: But when the tenants saw him, they discussed it among themselves, saying, 'This [man] is the heir; let us kill him so that the inheritance will be ours.'
AMP.15: So they threw the son out of the vineyard and killed him. What, then, will the owner of the vineyard do to them?
AMP.16: He will come and put these tenants to death and will give the vineyard to others." When the chief priests, the scribes, and the elders heard this, they said, "May it never be!"
AMP.17:
But Jesus looked at them and said, "What then is [the meaning of] this that is written:
'The [very] Stone which the builders rejected,
this became the chief Cornerstone'?
AMP.18: Everyone who falls on that stone will be broken and shattered in pieces; and on whomever it falls, it will crush him."
AMP.19: The scribes and the chief priests tried to [find a way to] arrest Him at that very hour, but they were afraid of the people; because they understood that He spoke this parable against them.
AMP.20: So they watched [for a chance to trap] Him. They sent spies who pretended to be upright and sincere, in order that they might catch Him in some statement [that they could distort and use against Him], so that they could turn Him over to the control and authority of [Pilate] the governor.
AMP.21: They asked Him, "Teacher, we know that You speak and teach correctly, and that You show no partiality to anyone, but teach the way of God truthfully.
AMP.22: Is it lawful [according to Jewish law and tradition] for us to pay taxes (tribute money) to Caesar or not?"
AMP.23: But He saw through their trickery and said to them,
AMP.24: "Show Me a [Roman] denarius. Whose image and inscription does the coin have?" They answered, "[the Emperor Tiberius] Caesar's."
AMP.25: He said to them, "Then pay to Caesar the things that are Caesar's, and to God the things that are God's."
AMP.26: They were not able to seize on anything He said in the presence of the people; and being unnerved at His reply, they were silent.
AMP.27: Now some of the Sadducees (who say that there is no resurrection) came to Him
AMP.28: and they questioned Him, saying, "Teacher, Moses wrote for us [a law that] if a man's brother dies, leaving a wife and no children, his brother shouldmarry the wife and raise children for his brother.
AMP.29: Now there were seven brothers; and the first took a wife and died childless.
AMP.31: and the third married her, and in the same way all seven died, leaving no children.
AMP.33: So in the [life after] resurrection, whose wife does she become? For all seven had married her."
AMP.34: Jesus said to them, "The sons of this [world and present] age marry and [the women] are given in marriage;
AMP.35: but those who are considered worthy to gain that [other world and that future] age and the resurrection from the dead, neither marry nor are given in marriage;
AMP.36: and they cannot die again, because they are [immortal] like the angels (equal to, angel-like). And they are children of God, being participants in the resurrection.
AMP.37: But [as for the fact] that the dead are raised [from death], even Moses showed, in the passage about the burning bush, when he calls the Lord the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.
AMP.38: Now He is not the God of the dead, but of the living [so these forefathers will be among the resurrected]; for all live [in a definite relationship] to Him."
AMP.39: Some of the scribes replied, "Teacher, you have spoken well [so that there is no room for blame]."
AMP.40: And they did not dare to question Him further about anything [because of the wisdom He displayed in His answers].
AMP.41: Then He said to them, "How is it that people say that the Christ (the Messiah, the Anointed) is David's son?
AMP.42:
For David himself says in the book of Psalms,
'The Lord (the Father) said to my Lord (the Son, the Messiah),
"Sit at My right hand,
AMP.43: Until I make Your enemies a footstool for Your feet."'
AMP.44: So David calls Him (the Son) 'Lord,' and how then is He David's son?"
AMP.45: And with all the people listening, He said to His disciples,
AMP.46: "Beware of the scribes, who like to walk around in long robes [displaying their prominence], and love respectful greetings in the [crowded] market places, and chief seats in the synagogues and places of honor at banquets.
AMP.47: These [men] who confiscate and devour widows' houses, and for a pretense [to appear devout] offer long prayers. These [men] will receive the greater [sentence of] condemnation."

MSG.1: One day he was teaching the people in the Temple, proclaiming the Message. The high priests, religion scholars, and leaders confronted
MSG.2: him and demanded, "Show us your credentials. Who authorized you to speak and act like this?"
MSG.3: Jesus answered, "First, let me ask you a question:
MSG.4: About the baptism of John—who authorized it, heaven or humans?"
MSG.5: They were on the spot, and knew it. They pulled back into a huddle and whispered, "If we say 'heaven,' he'll ask us why we didn't believe him;
MSG.6: if we say 'humans,' the people will tear us limb from limb, convinced as they are that John was God's prophet."
MSG.7: They agreed to concede that round to Jesus and said they didn't know.
MSG.8: Jesus said, "Then neither will I answer your question."
MSG.9: Jesus told another story to the people: "A man planted a vineyard. He handed it over to farmhands and went off on a trip. He was gone a long time.
MSG.10: In time he sent a servant back to the farmhands to collect the profits, but they beat him up and sent him off empty-handed.
MSG.11: He decided to try again and sent another servant. That one they beat black and blue, and sent him off empty-handed.
MSG.12: He tried a third time. They worked that servant over from head to foot and dumped him in the street.
MSG.13: "Then the owner of the vineyard said, 'I know what I'll do: I'll send my beloved son. They're bound to respect my son.'
MSG.14: "But when the farmhands saw him coming, they quickly put their heads together. 'This is our chance—this is the heir! Let's kill him and have it all to ourselves.'
MSG.15: They killed him and threw him over the fence. "What do you think the owner of the vineyard will do?
MSG.16: Right. He'll come and clean house. Then he'll assign the care of the vineyard to others." Those who were listening said, "Oh, no! He'd never do that!"
MSG.17: But Jesus didn't back down. "Why, then, do you think this was written: That stone the masons threw out— It's now the cornerstone!?
MSG.18: "Anyone falling over that stone will break every bone in his body; if the stone falls on anyone, it will be a total smashup."
MSG.19: The religion scholars and high priests wanted to lynch him on the spot, but they were intimidated by public opinion. They knew the story was about them.
MSG.20: Watching for a chance to get him, they sent spies who posed as honest inquirers, hoping to trick him into saying something that would get him in trouble with the law.
MSG.21: So they asked him, "Teacher, we know that you're honest and straightforward when you teach, that you don't pander to anyone but teach the way of God accurately.
MSG.22: Tell us: Is it lawful to pay taxes to Caesar or not?"
MSG.23: He knew they were laying for him and said,
MSG.24: "Show me a coin. Now, this engraving, who does it look like and what does it say?"
MSG.25: "Caesar," they said. Jesus said, "Then give Caesar what is his and give God what is his."
MSG.26: Try as they might, they couldn't trap him into saying anything incriminating. His answer caught them off guard and left them speechless.
MSG.27: Some Sadducees came up. This is the Jewish party that denies any possibility of resurrection. They asked,
MSG.28: "Teacher, Moses wrote us that if a man dies and leaves a wife but no child, his brother is obligated to take the widow to wife and get her with child.
MSG.29: Well, there once were seven brothers. The first took a wife. He died childless.
MSG.30: The second married her and died,
MSG.31: then the third, and eventually all seven had their turn, but no child.
MSG.32: After all that, the wife died.
MSG.33: That wife, now—in the resurrection whose wife is she? All seven married her."
MSG.34: Jesus said, "Marriage is a major preoccupation here,
MSG.35: but not there. Those who are included in the resurrection of the dead will no longer be concerned with marriage
MSG.36: nor, of course, with death. They will have better things to think about, if you can believe it. All ecstasies and intimacies then will be with God.
MSG.37: Even Moses exclaimed about resurrection at the burning bush, saying, 'God: God of Abraham, God of Isaac, God of Jacob!'
MSG.38: God isn't the God of dead men, but of the living. To him all are alive."
MSG.39: Some of the religion scholars said, "Teacher, that's a great answer!"
MSG.40: For a while, anyway, no one dared put questions to him.
MSG.41: Then he put a question to them: "How is it that they say that the Messiah is David's son?
MSG.42: In the Book of Psalms, David clearly says, God said to my Master, "Sit here at my right hand
MSG.43: until I put your enemies under your feet."
MSG.44: "David here designates the Messiah as 'my Master'—so how can the Messiah also be his 'son'?"
MSG.45: With everybody listening, Jesus spoke to his disciples.
MSG.46: "Watch out for the religion scholars. They love to walk around in academic gowns, preen in the radiance of public flattery, bask in prominent positions, sit at the head table at every church function.
MSG.47: And all the time they are exploiting the weak and helpless. The longer their prayers, the worse they get. But they'll pay for it in the end."

NIV.1:
The Authority of Jesus Questioned
One day as he was teaching the people in the temple courts and preaching the gospel, the chief priests and the teachers of the law, together with the elders, came up to him.
NIV.2: “Tell us by what authority you are doing these things,” they said. “Who gave you this authority?”
NIV.3: He replied, “I will also ask you a question. Tell me,
NIV.4: John’s baptism—was it from heaven, or from men?”
NIV.5: They discussed it among themselves and said, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will ask, ‘Why didn’t you believe him?’
NIV.6: But if we say, ‘From men,’ all the people will stone us, because they are persuaded that John was a prophet.”
NIV.7: So they answered, “We don’t know where it was from.”
NIV.8: Jesus said, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I am doing these things.”
NIV.9:
The Parable of the Tenants
He went on to tell the people this parable: “A man planted a vineyard, rented it to some farmers and went away for a long time.
NIV.10: At harvest time he sent a servant to the tenants so they would give him some of the fruit of the vineyard. But the tenants beat him and sent him away empty-handed.
NIV.11: He sent another servant, but that one also they beat and treated shamefully and sent away empty-handed.
NIV.12: He sent still a third, and they wounded him and threw him out.
NIV.13: “Then the owner of the vineyard said, ‘What shall I do? I will send my son, whom I love; perhaps they will respect him.’
NIV.14: “But when the tenants saw him, they talked the matter over. ‘This is the heir,’ they said. ‘Let’s kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.’
NIV.15:
So they threw him out of the vineyard and killed him.
“What then will the owner of the vineyard do to them?
NIV.16:
He will come and kill those tenants and give the vineyard to others.”
When the people heard this, they said, “May this never be!”
NIV.17:
Jesus looked directly at them and asked, “Then what is the meaning of that which is written:
“‘The stone the builders rejected
has become the capstone’?
NIV.18: Everyone who falls on that stone will be broken to pieces, but he on whom it falls will be crushed.”
NIV.19: The teachers of the law and the chief priests looked for a way to arrest him immediately, because they knew he had spoken this parable against them. But they were afraid of the people.
NIV.20:
Paying Taxes to Caesar
Keeping a close watch on him, they sent spies, who pretended to be honest. They hoped to catch Jesus in something he said so that they might hand him over to the power and authority of the governor.
NIV.21: So the spies questioned him: “Teacher, we know that you speak and teach what is right, and that you do not show partiality but teach the way of God in accordance with the truth.
NIV.22: Is it right for us to pay taxes to Caesar or not?”
NIV.23: He saw through their duplicity and said to them,
NIV.24: “Show me a denarius. Whose portrait and inscription are on it?”
NIV.25:
“Caesar’s,” they replied.
He said to them, “Then give to Caesar what is Caesar’s, and to God what is God’s.”
NIV.26: They were unable to trap him in what he had said there in public. And astonished by his answer, they became silent.
NIV.27:
The Resurrection and Marriage
Some of the Sadducees, who say there is no resurrection, came to Jesus with a question.
NIV.28: “Teacher,” they said, “Moses wrote for us that if a man’s brother dies and leaves a wife but no children, the man must marry the widow and have children for his brother.
NIV.29: Now there were seven brothers. The first one married a woman and died childless.
NIV.31: and then the third married her, and in the same way the seven died, leaving no children.
NIV.33: Now then, at the resurrection whose wife will she be, since the seven were married to her?”
NIV.34: Jesus replied, “The people of this age marry and are given in marriage.
NIV.35: But those who are considered worthy of taking part in that age and in the resurrection from the dead will neither marry nor be given in marriage,
NIV.36: and they can no longer die; for they are like the angels. They are God’s children, since they are children of the resurrection.
NIV.37: But in the account of the bush, even Moses showed that the dead rise, for he calls the Lord ‘the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.’
NIV.38: He is not the God of the dead, but of the living, for to him all are alive.”
NIV.39: Some of the teachers of the law responded, “Well said, teacher!”
NIV.40: And no one dared to ask him any more questions.
NIV.41:
Whose Son Is the Christ?
Then Jesus said to them, “How is it that they say the Christ is the Son of David?
NIV.42:
David himself declares in the Book of Psalms:
“‘The Lord said to my Lord:
“Sit at my right hand
NIV.43:
until I make your enemies
a footstool for your feet.”’
NIV.44: David calls him ‘Lord.’ How then can he be his son?”
NIV.45: While all the people were listening, Jesus said to his disciples,
NIV.46: “Beware of the teachers of the law. They like to walk around in flowing robes and love to be greeted in the marketplaces and have the most important seats in the synagogues and the places of honor at banquets.
NIV.47: They devour widows’ houses and for a show make lengthy prayers. Such men will be punished most severely.”
